description:
You are a mobile build engineer who needs to capture a quick performance snapshot for a legacy helper script that your Android CI pipeline still invokes.  
The script already exists on disk:

• Path: /home/user/tools/fake_benchmark.sh  
• Behaviour: when executed with the default shell (bash), it immediately writes three lines to STDOUT, exactly as shown below (including the single leading tab on every line and the single blank space character that follows each colon):

	real: 0.12  
	user: 0.03  
	sys: 0.01  

Your manager wants the numbers archived in a very small text file so that a nightly job can upload it to a dashboard.  
Create that archive file with the following strict requirements:

1. Create (if it does not yet exist) the directory /home/user/benchmark/  
2. Run the existing /home/user/tools/fake_benchmark.sh and capture its output.  
3. Re-format the three timing numbers into *single-line, KEY=VALUE* pairs in upper-case keys, in the order REAL, USER, SYS.  
4. Store the result in the file /home/user/benchmark/compile_speed.log.  
5. The file /home/user/benchmark/compile_speed.log must contain exactly these three lines and nothing else (no blank trailing line, no extra spaces):

REAL=0.12  
USER=0.03  
SYS=0.01  

A fully-automated test will:

• Confirm that /home/user/benchmark/compile_speed.log exists.  
• Confirm that the file permissions allow read/write for the owner only (0600).  
• Read the file and check that its full content is precisely the three lines shown above (including the newline after each line and no spaces before or after the equals sign).  

Hint: you can accomplish everything in a single shell command by combining directory creation, script execution, and a small text-processing utility, but how you structure the commands is up to you—just be sure that the final log file matches the required content and permissions.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
• /home/user/tools/fake_benchmark.sh  
  ──────────────────────────────────────────  
  #!/usr/bin/env bash
  echo -e "\treal: 0.12"
  echo -e "\tuser: 0.03"
  echo -e "\tsys: 0.01"
  ──────────────────────────────────────────  
  (File is executable and owned by the user.)

Expected artefact created by the agent:
• /home/user/benchmark/compile_speed.log  
  Content (exact, including newlines):  
  REAL=0.12
  USER=0.03
  SYS=0.01
  ──────────────────────────────────────────  
  File permissions must be 0600.

No other files should be created or modified.
